title: Users may be unable to view the device timeline in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint
impact: Users may have been unable to view the device timeline in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint.

updates.content:
Title: Some users may be encountering issues with the Microsoft Defender for Endpoint device timeline

User impact: Users may be encountering issues with the Microsoft Defender for Endpoint device timeline.

Current status: We're reviewing service monitoring telemetry to isolate the root cause and develop a remediation plan.
 
Scope of impact: Impact is specific to some Microsoft Defender for Endpoint users viewing device timelines.
 
Next update by: Thursday, March 12, 2026, at 4:30 PM UTC

Title: Users may be unable to view the device timeline in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint

User impact: Users may have been unable to view the device timeline in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint.

Final status: We've identified that there was an issue with our internal logging mechanism responsible for logging and communicating data for the device timeline in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint which blocked some requests from completing, caused a retry loop for those requests, and resulted in impact. While we continue to investigate the root cause of this issue, we've redirected traffic to an alternate portion of storage infrastructure that contains a healthy version of our logging mechanism and confirmed through service health telemetry that the impact was remediated.

Scope of impact: Any user that attempted to view the device timeline in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint may have been impacted.

Start time: Thursday, March 12, 2026, at 12:30 PM UTC

End time: Thursday, March 12, 2026, at 2:30 PM UTC

Root cause: An issue with our internal logging mechanism responsible for logging and communicating data for the device timeline in Microsoft Defender for Endpoint blocked some requests from completing, caused a retry loop for those requests, and resulted in impact.

Next steps: 
 - We're continuing to narrow down the reason that our logging mechanism was blocking requests from completing, so that we can correct the issue before balancing the request traffic back to the affected infrastructure.

This is the final update for the event.
